commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Lesson 36 — Edge Detection (#193)

* WIP: Add Lesson 36 — Edge Detection (in progress)

Sobel edge detection on G-buffer (depth + normals via MRT) and stencil-based
X-ray vision with depth_fail_op working. Shadow artifacts on spheres still
need debugging.

Working:
- Sobel edge detection (depth, normal, combined modes)
- Stencil X-ray ghost silhouettes (COMPAREOP_NOT_EQUAL fix)
- Debug G-buffer view (V key)
- Edge source cycling (E key)
- WASD + Space/Shift camera controls
- 6 diagrams, README, skill file

Known issues:
- Sphere shadows have holes/artifacts (depth bias tuning needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* WIP: Fix sphere shadows with CULLMODE_NONE + slope depth bias

- Removed hardware-only depth bias (caused holes in sphere shadows)
- Tried CULLMODE_FRONT (fixed spheres but broke cube shadows via peter panning)
- Settled on CULLMODE_NONE + slope-scaled bias (constant=1.5, slope=2.0)
- Sphere and cube shadows now correct

Remaining issues:
- X-ray target objects have casts_shadow=false, so no shadows when visible
- Dark circle artifact on one sphere (self-shadow at steep angle)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix sphere winding order, enable x-ray target shadows

- Reversed sphere triangle winding from (a, b, a+1) to (a, a+1, b)
  to produce CCW faces when viewed from outside, matching the cube
  convention. Fixes inverted lighting on all spheres.
- Enabled casts_shadow=true for x-ray target objects so they cast
  shadows like all other opaque geometry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix 7 stale comments to match implementation

- stencil>0 → stencil!=0 (NOT_EQUAL, not GREATER)
- Add missing Space/Shift camera controls to header
- model*view → view*model for normal transform order
- Update sphere vertex count comment (48+1)*(72+1)=3577
- X-ray targets now cast shadows (comment matched old behavior)
- GREATER → NOT_EQUAL in ghost pass render comment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Lesson 36 — Edge Detection

Two post-processing outline techniques using G-buffer data:

1. Sobel edge detection on depth and view-space normals via MRT,
   with sky masking and smoothstep thresholding
2. Stencil-based X-ray vision using depth_fail_op to reveal objects
   behind occluders as Fresnel ghost silhouettes with additive blending

Five-pass rendering: shadow map, G-buffer MRT (color + normals + D24S8),
fullscreen edge composite, stencil mark, and ghost render.

New APIs covered:
- MRT render pass with 2 color targets (lit color + view-space normals)
- SDL_SetGPUStencilReference for stencil compare reference
- SDL_DrawGPUPrimitives for fullscreen triangle from SV_VertexID
- depth_fail_op = INCREMENT_AND_WRAP for occluded-object marking
- Additive blending (ONE + ONE) for ghost glow accumulation

Files added:
- lessons/gpu/36-edge-detection/ — full lesson with 12 HLSL shaders
- .claude/skills/edge-detection/SKILL.md — reusable skill
- scripts/forge_diagrams/gpu_diagrams.py — 6 lesson diagrams

Also updates root README.md, PLAN.md to integrate the new lesson.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #193 feedback: diagram labels, defensive checks

- Use full INCREMENT_AND_WRAP name in X-ray diagrams (2 locations)
- Fix JFA cost to ceil(log2(max(w,h))) in outline comparison
- Add NULL check for SDL_BeginGPUCopyPass in upload helper
- Silence unused argc/argv warnings when FORGE_CAPTURE not defined

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR feedback: NULL checks, diagram fixes, redundant code removal

- Return NULL from upload_gpu_buffer on SDL_SubmitGPUCommandBuffer failure
- NULL-check all 5 SDL_BeginGPURenderPass return values
- Use state->light_dir instead of redundant recalculation in SDL_AppIterate
- Rename diagram_gbuffer_layout to diagram_edge_detection_gbuffer_layout
- Add front/side depth boundary to depth-only diagram panel
- Fix stencil-expansion width description from "Uniform" to "Approximate"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: replace all innerHTML assignments with setHTML/appendHTML helpers

Add setHTML(el, html) and appendHTML(el, html) DOM helpers that use
DOMParser instead of innerHTML, eliminating all 99 remaining
UNSAFE_VAR_ASSIGNMENT warnings from the Firefox addons-linter.

- popup.js: 125 innerHTML= → setHTML(); 4 innerHTML+= → appendHTML();
  empty innerHTML= → replaceChildren()
- qr-generator.js: single innerHTML= → createElement + replaceChildren()

Firefox validator result: 0 errors, 0 warnings (was 0 errors, 99 warnings)

commit.message:
feat: ExecutionDriver contract and LocalExecutionDriver (#50)

* feat: add ExecutionDriver contract and LocalExecutionDriver (#42)

Introduce an ExecutionDriver interface for sandboxed command execution
and file operations, with a LocalExecutionDriver implementation that
enforces path validation against traversal attacks. Includes config,
value object, and comprehensive Pest tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on LocalExecutionDriver

- Ensure base path directory exists in constructor, throw on resolution failure
- Add error checking for mkdir() and file_put_contents() in writeFile()
- Guard against empty old_string in editFile()
- Check grep exit code 2 for errors instead of silently swallowing
- Fix path boundary bypass (e.g. /tmp/foo vs /tmp/foobar) in resolvePath and resolvePathForWrite
- Validate glob patterns against path traversal and filter results to base path
- Coerce filesize() return to int in listDirectory()
- Fix test for listDirectory exception message
- Add test for empty old_string in editFile

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

AVX-512 delta-net SIMD + fused norm+LM head on single chip (~115ms/tok)

- AVX-512 intrinsics for delta-net recurrence: fused decay+dot+update+output
  passes halve memory traffic, explicit SIMD for 128-element inner loops
  (delta-net: 9.3ms → 4.3ms per token)
- Fuse output RMSNorm + LM head matmul as traced chain on chip 0,
  eliminating host read/write round-trip and chip 1 dependency
- Use single device (chip 0 only) instead of two unit meshes,
  reducing per-EnqueueWrite driver overhead (reswr: 12ms → 1ms)
- Net improvement: ~140ms → ~115ms per token (~8.7 tok/s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add microplans plugin (#13)

Adds a new microplans plugin that implements a structured pattern for
scaling AI code generation over multi-step roadmaps. The plugin provides
a master plan format for tracking progress across sessions, an 8-step
execution loop (plan, ask questions, incorporate answers, implement,
commit, repeat), and a microplanner agent that researches the codebase
and produces detailed implementation plans with outstanding questions
for user review.

The plugin includes two skills (using-microplans for the execution loop,
writing-microplans for the document format) and one Opus agent
(microplanner) that adapts to whatever domain role is assigned in the
delegation prompt.

---------

feat: add explain, data-lineage, orient plugins + rename to harness-kit (#1)

* feat: add explain and data-lineage plugins

Two new skills for the marketplace:

- /explain — structured code explainer with adaptive depth (file, directory,
  symbol, or concept). 6-section output: summary, key components, connections,
  patterns, gotchas, entry points for change. Project-aware via CLAUDE.md.

- /data-lineage — column-level data lineage tracer for heterogeneous stacks
  (SQL, Kafka, Spark, JDBC, ORM). Traces upstream/downstream with confidence
  ratings per hop. Generates SVG diagrams.

Both plugins have no external dependencies. SKILL.md files use natural language
search instructions (not hardcoded grep) for tool-agnostic execution.

Also updates CLAUDE.md repo structure diagram and README plugin table.

commit.message:
fix(settings): always revert gated style fields to defaults on sheet close

Remove conditional guard on DisposableEffect revert — when persistedStyle
already had defaults, the check skipped the revert while onStyleChanged
had pushed preview values during the session, causing alternating-close
bug. Now unconditionally emits persistedStyle with gated fields forced
to schema defaults.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.5.7: fix @feedback during planning state routing to planner instead of crashing

@feedback during planning previously crashed with "Invalid transition: planning →
iterating" because it always routed through the post-build iteration handler.
Now detects session state and delegates to the planner flow during planning
(plan refinement), while preserving the iteration path for post-build feedback.

commit.message:
fix: 避免导入后 Ace 空值清空脚本

导入或程序化覆盖后，主编辑器在 Ace 尚未完成 hydration 时可能先回传空字符串，导致预览前同步把非空脚本误清空。为此给主编辑器增加待 hydration 保护，只在初始化空值阶段跳过覆盖，保留用户后续真实清空脚本的能力。

commit.message:
fix: GPU kernel bugs, DRY consolidation, dead code cleanup (#110)

* fix: GPU kernel bugs, eliminate dead/duplicate code, fix scripts and docs

Bug fixes:
- parlz_resolve.wgsl: replace hardcoded 65535u Y-stride with params.z
  (dispatch_width) across all 4 entry points — fixes incorrect global
  indices for large inputs requiring 2D dispatch
- sortlz_ops.wgsl: verify match bytes from position 0 instead of assuming
  4-byte hash equality, add minimum match length check — fixes hash
  collision false matches
- stages.rs: replace 2 .unwrap() panics on pre_entropy_len with proper
  error propagation via .ok_or(PzError::InvalidInput)
- stages.rs: fix decode_recoil_payload() to use resolve_thread_count(0)

DRY consolidation:
- Unify LzToken enum into lz77.rs (remove duplicates from sortlz/parlz)
- Add lz77::serialize_matches() / deserialize_matches() shared helpers
- Remove lz77_compress_with_backend(), merge into lz77_matches_with_backend()
- Extract parse_rans_stream_header(), parse_recoil_payload(),
  decode_rans_stream_cpu() from duplicated rANS decode logic
- Add FrequencyTable::recompute_totals() method

Dead code cleanup:
- Delete kernels/lz77_local.wgsl (198 lines, never loaded)
- Remove unused stage_huffman_encode_webgpu()
- Gate gpu_cost/gpu_common behind #[cfg(feature = "webgpu")]
- Gate Backend import in demux.rs and deserialize_matches behind webgpu

Script fixes: bench.sh filter pattern, fetch-silesia.sh paths,
gpu-experiment-bench.sh sample path + dead pipeline, trace-pipeline.sh ref

Doc fixes: DESIGN.md paths/status, gpu-experiments-wave2.md deleted file refs

19 files changed, ~280 lines net reduction. All 722 tests pass, zero
clippy warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: fix stale return-value list in parse_rans_stream_header doc comment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: disable SSE2 rANS decode dispatch — scalar is 32% faster

Benchmarking revealed the SSE2 4-way rANS decode path (rans_decode_4way_sse2)
runs at 677 MB/s vs the scalar path's 999 MB/s — a 32% regression. The SSE2
path only accelerates the multiply step, but the actual bottleneck is the
scalar gather (slot → symbol → freq/cum lookup). The CPU's out-of-order
engine overlaps the 4 independent scalar lane computations better than
waiting for SSE2 extract serialization.

Disable the SSE2 runtime dispatch so scalar runs by default. The SSE2
implementation is preserved in simd.rs for future work. A proper SIMD
rANS decoder would need ryg_rans-style techniques: merged slot-indexed
tables (freq+bias per slot), PSHUFB branchless renormalization, single
shared bitstream, and SSE4.1 _mm_mullo_epi32.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add ryg_rans-style merged slot-indexed table for rANS decode

Replaces the 2-hop gather pattern (slot→symbol→freq/cum) with a single
merged SlotEntry table where each slot contains both freq and bias
(slot - cum[sym]) packed into a u32. The 4-way interleaved decode
path now uses rans_decode_4way_slot() which performs one table lookup
per lane instead of three.

Phase 1 of 3 in the ryg_rans-style SIMD decoder implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add shared-stream rANS codec (ryg_rans Phase 2)

Add encode_shared_stream / decode_shared_stream API where all N
interleaved lanes push renormalization words to a single shared word
stream instead of per-lane streams. This is the prerequisite for
PSHUFB branchless SIMD renormalization (Phase 3).

Wire format: [scale_bits][freq_table][num_states][final_states]
[total_words][shared_word_stream][8-byte zero padding]

Pipeline integration:
- RANS_SHARED_STREAM_FLAG (bit 29) in per-stream header
- rans_shared_stream option in CompressOptions
- Decode dispatch in stages.rs routes to decode_shared_stream()
- Backward compatible: old interleaved/basic payloads still decode

Scalar shared-stream decode: 558 MB/s (vs 1.14 GB/s interleaved).
Expected — the SIMD path in Phase 3 will eliminate the serial
renormalization bottleneck.

Phase 2 of 3 in the ryg_rans-style SIMD decoder implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: PSHUFB SIMD decoder + batched scalar shared-stream (ryg_rans Phase 3)

Add SSSE3+SSE4.1 branchless PSHUFB rANS decoder that combines:
- Merged slot table (Phase 1: eliminates 2-hop gather)
- Shared word stream (Phase 2: single pointer for all lanes)
- PSHUFB shuffle-based renorm (branchless word routing via 4-bit mask)
- SSE4.1 _mm_mullo_epi32 (4-lane u32 multiply in one instruction)

Key finding: the SIMD path (432 MiB/s) is 2.7x SLOWER than optimized
scalar because the scalar gather bottleneck (slot2sym + slot_table
lookups require extracting states to scalars and repacking) dominates
over the branchless renorm savings. The SIMD implementation is preserved
but disabled for future AVX2 work where _mm256_i32gather_epi32 will
eliminate the gather overhead.

Optimized the scalar shared-stream decoder to use batched 4-way inner
loop (matching rans_decode_4way_slot structure). Result: 1.18 GiB/s —
5% faster than the interleaved path (1.13 GiB/s) due to better cache
locality from a single word pointer.

Performance summary (1 MB, 100-symbol skewed distribution):
  Baseline (pre-slot-table):     999 MB/s
  Interleaved + slot table:    1,127 MiB/s (+13%)
  Shared-stream scalar:        1,183 MiB/s (+18%)
  Shared-stream SIMD:            432 MiB/s (disabled, awaits AVX2)

Phase 3 of 3 in the ryg_rans-style SIMD decoder implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add AVX2 gather rANS decoder, --rans-shared-stream CLI flag, side-by-side benchmarks

Add AVX2 hardware gather variant of the shared-stream PSHUFB decoder
(rans_decode_4way_shared_avx2) to test whether _mm_i32gather_epi32
eliminates the scalar gather bottleneck. Benchmark results show it
does not — AVX2 gather has ~12 cycle latency matching 4 scalar loads:

  scalar batched:    1.145 GiB/s  ← winner
  SSSE3 PSHUFB:      429 MiB/s   (2.67x slower)
  AVX2 hw gather:    422 MiB/s   (2.71x slower)

Both SIMD dispatches are disabled; scalar remains default. The SIMD
implementations are preserved for reference and benchmarking via
force_scalar/force_ssse3/force_avx2 helpers.

Also adds --rans-shared-stream CLI flag and side-by-side criterion
benchmarks for all three implementations.

Review fixes: SIMD padding on owned Vec (was UB), SSSE3 safety doc
(4→8 bytes), dead code cleanup, clippy suppressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix Azure AD AADSTS70011 scope error in sandbox Swagger UI

Azure AD rejects SMART on FHIR scope strings (patient/*.read, fhirUser,
system/*.read) because they are not valid URI-form scopes. Change all specs
and the initOAuth config to use standard OIDC scopes (openid, profile,
offline_access) for authorization code flows and api://client-id/.default
for client credentials flows. These work with any Azure AD multi-tenant app
without requiring custom scope registration.

commit.message:
Add CLI subcommands: converse, status, resume, close

Two-phase arg parser separates global flags (--config, --verbose) from
subcommand + subcommand-specific flags. status and close exit early
without PID lock or daemon startup. converse and resume gracefully
fall back when daemon is already running — write to DB and let the
running daemon pick up the change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add page-aware CaseFileChunker for case file text (Phase L3.1)

Introduces casefile/chunker.py with type-specific chunking strategies:
PDF (form-feed page boundaries), email (mbox message separator),
spreadsheet (sheet headings), and generic paragraph fallback.
Default 1000 max tokens for precise case file retrieval.
PDFExtractor now uses \f page separator instead of \n\n.
52 new tests.

refactor(server): split rooms.ts, proxy iTunes via server with caching

- Extract shared types/interfaces to server/src/types.ts
- Extract game mutation logic to server/src/game.ts
- Slim down RoomManager to auth/routing layer with getPlayer() helper
- Add emitGameOver() helper and clean up disconnect handler in index.ts
- Fix tsconfig: use module/moduleResolution Node16, add rootDir

Fix iTunes rate limiting by proxying requests through the server:
- Add /api/preview endpoint with in-memory cache (server/src/preview.ts)
- Client calls /api/preview instead of iTunes directly (no CORS issues)
- Add Vite dev proxy for /api/ → localhost:3001
- Compute preview target dynamically from playerCount × rounds

Add MIT LICENSE

Fix backup: detect running containers, validate copies, fix postgres permissions

  (postgres could be running even when web server wasn't responding)
- Check rsync exit code and verify backup size after copy
- chown postgres backup files so they're readable without root
- Add pre-copy container check before database backup

commit.message:
feat: Cloudflare Worker によるピンポイント dispatch アーキテクチャ実装

## 概要
cron ポーリング（15分おき）から CF Worker ベースのピンポイント dispatch に移行。
GitHub Actions 起動回数を 1日~31回 → ~12回（レース数分）に削減。

## 変更ファイル

### 新規: workers/dispatcher.js
- Cloudflare Worker（毎分 cron）実装
- GitHub Pages から race_schedule.json を fetch
- notify_at が「今から 0〜60 秒以内」のレースを検出
- KV でベキ等性保証（同一 race_id の多重 dispatch 防止、TTL=4h）
- GitHub API POST /dispatches → keiba_dispatch.yml を発火
- HTTP fetch でのドライラン確認機能付き

### 新規: workers/wrangler.toml
- cron: "* * * * *"（毎分）
- KV バインディング（DISPATCH_KV）
- 環境変数: GH_OWNER, GH_REPO, PAGES_BASE_URL
- Secret: GH_TOKEN（wrangler secret put で設定）

### 新規: .github/workflows/keiba_dispatch.yml
- workflow_dispatch トリガー（race_id input 受け取り）
- concurrency で同一 race_id の二重実行防止
- timeout-minutes: 20（単一レース処理で十分）

### 変更: src/scheduler/runner.py
- run_for_race_id(race_id): CF Worker dispatch 用、time-window チェックなし
  race_schedule.json から race_name/start_time を補完
- export_race_schedule(date): CF Worker 用 JSON を docs/ に書き出す
  {date, race_id, race_name, race_number, jyo_name, start_time, notify_at}

### 変更: src/scheduler/daily_batch.py
- --morning: 既存の予想ページ生成後に export_race_schedule() を自動呼び出し
- --notify-race RACE_ID: CF Worker からの dispatch を受け run_for_race_id() 実行
- --export-schedule: スケジュール JSON 単独エクスポート

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
LIMIT_INVALID 에러 수정: 동시성 대폭 축소 + 배치 간 딜레이 추가

- 기본값 변경: parallelChunks 10→3, downloadQueue 500→50, parallelDownloads 3→2
- 최소 세그먼트 크기 512KB로 상향 (세그먼트 수 감소)
- 동시 요청 하드캡 5개로 제한
- 배치 간 150ms 딜레이 추가
- HTTP 400 (LIMIT_INVALID) 재시도 지원 (2초 백오프)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix chromagram false-positive detection on mic noise

Problems fixed:
1. Silence threshold too loose (-58 dB) — mic background noise easily
   exceeded it, causing phantom notes to be detected constantly.
   Raised to -48 dB and narrowed the processing window from 40 dB
   to 30 dB below peak.

2. No uniformity guard — in noise all 12 pitch-class buckets are roughly
   equal, so relative thresholding always flagged 5-8 "notes". Added a
   check: max chroma must be ≥ 3× median chroma; noise fails this,
   real chords pass it easily.

3. History advanced even during silence — silent frames still incremented
   the counter, so the progress bar ran to 100% and reset in a loop.
   Now history only accumulates when ≥ 2 real notes are detected.
   Four consecutive silent frames (~0.6 s) clear stale history.

4. History reset on every failed chord attempt — caused the progress bar
   to keep cycling. Replaced with a true sliding window: after the
   window is full, every new frame tries to lock without resetting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add bar menu page

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
